name: Pumble
one_liner: Pumble is a free team chat and collaboration app (Slack alternative) that exposes a REST API via an installable API-keys addon for sending messages, managing channels, and reactions.
access_notes: The API-keys addon is installable on all Pumble plans (including the free plan) by Owner/Admin/Member roles. After installing the addon, a user self-generates an API key instantly in-app (or via /api-keys generate) with no payment or human approval, then authorizes it in the Swagger docs.
api_notes: REST API documented via Swagger. Supported operations are a handful: send message, reply to message, delete message, add reaction, create channel, list channel messages (bot must be member for private channels), list channels/DMs, and list users. Rate limit up to 1000 requests/minute per user. A Node.js SDK (pumble-node-sdk / pumble-sdk) also exists for building Pumble apps.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server found. Community/third-party MCP servers exist (e.g. shoutkol/pumble-mcp-server on GitHub/LobeHub) plus proxy MCP offerings from Zapier, Pipedream, and viaSocket that wrap the Pumble API.
main_blocker: None significant: public REST API with self-serve API keys on any plan. Main limitation is narrow API surface (small set of messaging operations) and no documented webhooks/event subscriptions for inbound triggers.

evidence.claim: API-keys addon available on all plans to Owner/Admin/Member; API key self-generated in-app; auth via API key; rate limit 1000 req/min per user; no webhooks mentioned.
Official Pumble API addon docs (Swagger/Scalar) hosted at cake.com marketplace listing available API actions.
Official Pumble Node.js SDK (pumble-node-sdk) published by CAKE-com for building Pumble apps.
Community Pumble MCP server (send messages, list channels/users, reactions) requiring PUMBLE_API_KEY, not vendor-shipped.

open_questions: Exact total operation count is not confirmed (Swagger page did not render fully). Whether Pumble offers a separate incoming-webhooks integration distinct from the REST API is unverified; the API-keys docs mention no event/webhook subscriptions.
